Series #1 — React from Beginner to Advanced
If you’re starting your frontend development journey, you’ve probably heard one question over and over:
“Should I learn React?”
The answer, in most cases, is yes.
React powers some of the world’s most-used applications and has become one of the most valuable skills for frontend developers. But before we start writing code, it’s important to understand what React actually is, why it was created, and what problems it solves.
In this article, we’ll build that foundation.
Before React Existed
Imagine you’re building a simple shopping website using only HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
The page displays:

• User profile

• Shopping cart

• Notifications

• Product list

• Search results
Now imagine the user adds one product to the cart.
Without React, JavaScript often requires you to:

• Find the cart element in the DOM.
